zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard Watkins於1569年11月25日出生在英國賀福爾波斯波利時，他的父親John Watkins24歲，母親Johana Baugh26歲。他和Martha Peede至少有3個兒子和2個女兒。

nameMeanings.name: Watkins
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Welsh: variant of Watkin , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Watkin
meanings: English and Welsh: from the personal name Watkin, a diminutive of Walter .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

historicEvents.title: Glorious Revolution
historicEvents.description: The Glorious Revolution brought the downfall of Catholic King James II and the reign of his Protestant daughter Mary and her Dutch husband, William III of Orange.

en.lifeSummary: When Richard Watkins was born on 25 November 1569, in Bosbury, Herefordshire, England, his father, John Watkins, was 24 and his mother, Johana Baugh, was 26. He had at least 3 sons and 2 daughters with Martha Peede.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard Watkins est né le 25 novembre 1569, à Bosbury, Herefordshire, Angleterre, son père, John Watkins, avait 24 ans et sa mère, Johana Baugh, avait 26 ans. Il a eu au moins 3 fils et 2 filles avec Martha Peede.

de.lifeSummary: Als Richard Watkins am 25. November 1569 in Bosbury, Herefordshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater John Watkins 24 und seine Mutter Johana Baugh 26. Er hatte mit Martha Peede mindestens 3 Söhne und 2 Töchter.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Watkins è nato il il 25 novembre 1569 nel Bosbury, Herefordshire, England, il suo padre, John Watkins, aveva 24 anni e il suo madre, Johana Baugh, aveva 26 anni. Lui avuto almeno 3 figli e 2 figlie con Martha Peede.

ja.lifeSummary: Richard Watkinsが1569年11月25日にイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアボスブリーで生まれた時，父John Watkinsは24歳，母Johana Baughは26歳であった。彼はMartha Peedeとの間に少なくとも3人の息子と2人の娘をもうけた。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard Watkins(이)가 1569년 11월 25일 영국 허퍼드실르 보스버이에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Watkins(은)는 24세였고, 그의 어머니 Johana Baugh(은)는 26세였다. 그는 Martha Peede(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Watkins nasceu em 25 de novembro de 1569, em Bosbury, Herefordshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, John Watkins, tinha 24 anos e sua mãe, Johana Baugh, tinha 26 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 3 filhos e 2 filhas com Martha Peede.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard Watkins родился 25 ноября 1569 г. в местности босбури, хирефордшир, Англия, его отцу по имени John Watkins было 24 года, а матери, которую звали Johana Baugh, 26. У него было по крайней мере 3 сына и 2 дочери от Martha Peede.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Richard Watkins nació el 25 de noviembre de 1569, en Bosbury, Herefordshire, Inglaterra, su papá, John Watkins, tenía 24 años y su mamá, Johana Baugh, tenía 26 años. Tuvo por lo menos 3 hijos y 2 hijas con Martha Peede.
